req.title: KYC refresh
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-015 defines three critical behavioural zones: (1) a valid KYC refresh submitted within the 365-day window must set customer.verified=true; (2) a refresh submitted with non-compliant data must return HTTP 403; (3) the exact boundary at day 365 must still be accepted, while day 366 must trigger restriction. We therefore need: one EP positive case (happy-path refresh), one NEG/DT case (compliance failure yields 403), and two BVA cases (day 364 inside window, day 366 outside window). We also add an STC case to confirm that a restricted account becomes fully active post-refresh. Fixtures and helpers are placeholders per the task instructions. Each test is self-contained via setup/teardown stubs and uses a dedicated test-scoped customer account to guarantee idempotency.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_within_window_sets_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed 180 days ago (within 365-day window)
KYC form payload passes all compliance rules
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant payload and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains a refreshed_at timestamp within the last 60 seconds
trace: R-FIN-015: customer shall complete KYC refresh every 365 days
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
from <helper_fn> import build_auth_header, assert_recent_timestamp

@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
def customer_180_days(<fixture_api_client>, <fixture_create_customer>):
    customer = <fixture_create_customer>(kyc_age_days=180)
    yield customer
    <fixture_create_customer>.cleanup(customer["id"])

def test_kyc_refresh_within_window_sets_verified(customer_180_days, <fixture_api_client>):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": customer_180_days["id"],
        "document_type": "passport",
        "document_number": "X123456789",
        "expiry_date": "2030-01-01"
    }
    headers = build_auth_header(customer_180_days["token"])
    response = <fixture_api_client>.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert_recent_timestamp(body["refreshed_at"], tolerance_seconds=60)

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed 180 days ago (within 365-day window)
KYC form payload contains a document flagged by the compliance engine (e.g. sanctioned document number)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with a non-compliant payload (sanctioned document number) and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error code referencing compliance failure
trace: R-FIN-015: error pathway 403 when submission fails compliance
R-FIN-015: verified state must not change on failed submission
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import build_auth_header, fetch_customer_verified_status

@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
def customer_compliance_fail(<fixture_api_client>, <fixture_create_customer>):
    customer = <fixture_create_customer>(kyc_age_days=180, verified=False)
    yield customer
    <fixture_create_customer>.cleanup(customer["id"])

def test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403(
    customer_compliance_fail, <fixture_api_client>
):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": customer_compliance_fail["id"],
        "document_type": "passport",
        "document_number": "SANCTIONED-DOC-001",
        "expiry_date": "2030-01-01"
    }
    headers = build_auth_header(customer_compliance_fail["token"])
    response = <fixture_api_client>.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "compliance" in body.get("error_code", "").lower()
    verified = fetch_customer_verified_status(
        <fixture_api_client>, customer_compliance_fail["id"], customer_compliance_fail["token"]
    )
    assert verified is False

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_at_day_365_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed exactly 365 days ago (boundary: last valid day of window)
KYC form payload passes all compliance rules
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant payload on day 365 since last refresh
expected: Account is NOT restricted (outbound transactions remain permitted)
trace: R-FIN-015: every 365 days — day 365 is still within the refresh window
R-FIN-015: accounts beyond refresh window restricted; day 365 is not beyond
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import build_auth_header, fetch_account_restriction

@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
def customer_365_days(<fixture_api_client>, <fixture_create_customer>):
    customer = <fixture_create_customer>(kyc_age_days=365)
    yield customer
    <fixture_create_customer>.cleanup(customer["id"])

def test_kyc_refresh_at_day_365_boundary_accepted(
    customer_365_days, <fixture_api_client>
):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": customer_365_days["id"],
        "document_type": "passport",
        "document_number": "V987654321",
        "expiry_date": "2031-06-15"
    }
    headers = build_auth_header(customer_365_days["token"])
    response = <fixture_api_client>.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    restriction = fetch_account_restriction(
        <fixture_api_client>, customer_365_days["id"], customer_365_days["token"]
    )
    assert restriction["outbound_blocked"] is False

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_at_day_366_account_restricted_before_and_unrestricted_after
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC was completed 366 days ago (one day beyond the window)
Account is currently in RESTRICTED state (inbound only)
KYC form payload passes all compliance rules
actions: Verify account is restricted before refresh (outbound blocked)
Verify account transitions to ACTIVE state (outbound permitted)
expected: Pre-refresh: account restricted, outbound transactions blocked
Post-refresh: customer.verified=true and outbound transactions unblocked
trace: R-FIN-015: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound only (day 366 is beyond)
R-FIN-015: successful refresh transitions account from RESTRICTED to ACTIVE
R-FIN-015: state transition RESTRICTED -> ACTIVE on verified=true
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import build_auth_header, fetch_account_restriction

@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
def customer_366_days(<fixture_api_client>, <fixture_create_customer>):
    customer = <fixture_create_customer>(kyc_age_days=366)
    yield customer
    <fixture_create_customer>.cleanup(customer["id"])

def test_kyc_refresh_at_day_366_account_restricted_before_and_unrestricted_after(
    customer_366_days, <fixture_api_client>
):
    cid = customer_366_days["id"]
    token = customer_366_days["token"]
    headers = build_auth_header(token)
    pre = fetch_account_restriction(<fixture_api_client>, cid, token)
    assert pre["outbound_blocked"] is True, "Account must be restricted before refresh"
    payload = {
        "customer_id": cid,
        "document_type": "national_id",
        "document_number": "NID-2024-00042",
        "expiry_date": "2032-03-20"
    }
    response = <fixture_api_client>.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    post = fetch_account_restriction(<fixture_api_client>, cid, token)
    assert post["outbound_blocked"] is False, "Account must be unrestricted after refresh"

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_missing_auth_returns_401
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with a valid body but without an Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
trace: R-FIN-015: precondition is authenticated — unauthenticated request must be rejected
R-FIN-015: decision table: auth=absent AND payload=valid => 401 Unauthorized
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_missing_auth_returns_401(<fixture_api_client>):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": "cust-anon-test-001",
        "document_type": "passport",
        "document_number": "A000000001",
        "expiry_date": "2033-01-01"
    }
    response = <fixture_api_client>.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 401
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    error_text = (body.get("error") or body.get("message") or "").lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["unauthorized", "authentication", "token"])
